rationale: In the citing paper, Van Heerde, Gijsbrechts, and Pauwels (2008) appears only as a methodological precedent in a robustness-check footnote (using pulse dummies to separate immediate versus enduring effects). The paper does not rely on this cited study for its core theory development, hypotheses, or main empirical identification strategy, which are built around context-effects literature and the PL tier introduction setting. Because the citation serves as a brief "similar approach" reference rather than a central intellectual input, its role is best classified as a passing mention.
